name: minitrino

metadata.name: minitrino
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A command line tool that makes it easy to run modular Trino environments locally.

documentation: # Minitrino A command line tool that makes it easy to run modular Trino environments locally. Compatible with Starburst versions 429-e and later. [![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/minitrino.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/minitrino) ![CLI Tests](https://github.com/jefflester/minitrino/actions/workflows/cli-tests.yaml/badge.svg) ![Library Tests](https://github.com/jefflester/minitrino/actions/workflows/lib-tests.yaml/badge.svg) [![Trino Slack](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?logo=slack&logoColor=959DA5&label=Slack&labelColor=333a41&message=join%20conversation&color=3AC358)](https://trinodb.io/slack.html) ----- **Latest Stable Release**: 2.2.2 ----- ## Quick Start To learn more about the CLI and underlying library, visit the following wiki pages: - [Installation and Upgrades](https://github.com/jefflester/minitrino/wiki/Installation-and-Upgrades) - [Workflow Examples](https://github.com/jefflester/minitrino/wiki/Workflow-Examples) - [Environment Variables and Config](https://github.com/jefflester/minitrino/wiki/Environment-Variables-and-Config) - [Build a Module](https://github.com/jefflester/minitrino/wiki/Build-a-Module)

modules.name: minitrino.errors
documentation: !usr/bin/env/python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: minitrino.errors.MinitrinoError
documentation: Generic Minitrino exception class.

classes.name: minitrino.errors.UserError
documentation:
An exception that Minitrino can handle and show to the user.

### Parameters
- `msg`: The message to log, and the raised exception's message.
- `hint_msg`: User hint (optional). Should help the user figure out how to
  solve the problem.

modules.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_lib_install

modules.name: minitrino.utils
documentation: !usr/bin/env/python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: minitrino.utils.handle_exception
documentation:
Handles a single exception. Wrapped by `@exception_handler` decorator.

### Parameters
- `error`: The exception object.
- `additional_msg`: An additional message to log, if any. Can be useful if
  handling a generic exception and you need to append a user-friendly
  message to the log.
- `skip_traceback`: If `True`, the traceback will not be printed to the
  user's terminal. Defaults to `True` for user errors, but it is `False`
  otherwise.

functions.name: minitrino.utils.exception_handler
documentation:
A decorator that handles unhandled exceptions. Why? A few reasons.

1. Inner functions still have the liberty to do try/catch and perform
   inner-function exception handling how they wish.
2. Functions that catch exceptions which need specialized handling can
   manually invoke the handle_exception() utility.
3. For all other generic exceptions and unhandled exceptions, they will be
   siphoned to the handle_exception() utility.

This is especially useful when decorating main/runner functions and class
constructors.

functions.name: minitrino.utils.check_daemon
documentation:
Checks if the Docker daemon is running. If an exception is thrown, it is
handled.

functions.name: minitrino.utils.check_lib
documentation: Checks if a Minitrino library exists.

functions.name: minitrino.utils.check_starburst_ver
documentation: Checks if a proper Starburst version is provided.

functions.name: minitrino.utils.check_version_requirements
documentation:
Checks for SEP version validity per version requirements specified in any
module(s).

functions.name: minitrino.utils.check_dependent_modules
documentation: Checks if any of the provided modules have module dependencies.

functions.name: minitrino.utils.generate_identifier
documentation:
Returns an 'object identifier' string used for creating log messages,
e.g. '[ID: 12345] [Name: trino]'.

### Parameters
- `identifiers`: Dictionary of "identifier_value": "identifier_key" pairs.

### Usage
```python
identifier = generate_identifier(
    {"ID": container.short_id, "Name": container.name}
) # Will Spit out -> "[ID: 12345] [Name: trino]"
```

functions.name: minitrino.utils.parse_key_value_pair
documentation:
Parses a key-value pair in string form and returns the resulting pair as
both a 2-element list. If the string cannot be split by "=", a
MinitrinoError is raised.

### Parameters
- `key_value_pair`: A string formatted as a key-value pair, i.e.
  `"STARBURST_VER=388-e"`.
- `err_type`: The exception to raise if an "=" delimiter is not in the
  key-value pair. Defaults to `MinitrinoError`.
- `key_to_upper`: If `True`, the key will be forced to uppercase.

### Return Values
- A list `[k, v]`, but will return `None` if the stripped input is an empty
  string.

functions.name: minitrino.utils.get_cli_ver
documentation: Returns the version of the Minitrino CLI.

functions.name: minitrino.utils.get_lib_ver
documentation:
Returns the version of the Minitrino library.

### Parameters
- `library_path`: The Minitrino library directory.

functions.name: minitrino.utils.validate_yes
documentation:
Validates 'yes' user input. Returns `True` if a 'yes' input is
detected.

classes.name: minitrino.utils.Logger

methods.name: minitrino.utils.Logger.log
documentation:
Logs messages to the user's console. Defaults to 'info' log level.

### Parameters
- `*args`: Messages to log.
- `level`: The level of the log message (info, warn, error, verbose).
- `stream`: If `True`, the logger will not apply a prefix to each line streamed
  to the console.

methods.name: minitrino.utils.Logger.prompt_msg
documentation:
Logs a prompt message and returns the user's input.

### Parameters
- `msg`: The prompt message

methods.name: minitrino.utils.Logger._format
documentation: Formats strings prior to displaying to the user.

Minitrino logging class. Outputs to the user's terminal using `click.echo()`
with color-coded levels and optional verbosity.

### Parameters
- `log_verbose`: If `True`, verbose messages will be logged; otherwise, they're
  suppressed.

### Attributes
- `INFO`, `WARN`, `ERROR`, `VERBOSE`: Log level configurations.

### Methods
- `log()`: Logs a message to the terminal with specified level and format.
- `info()`, `warn()`, `error()`, `verbose()`: Convenience methods for logging.
- `prompt_msg()`: Logs a prompt message and returns user input.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_remove.remove_items

Removes Docker items. If no labels are passed in, all Minitrino
resources are removed. If label(s) are passed in, the removal is limited to
the passed in labels.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_remove.try_get_image_tag

modules.name: minitrino.components
documentation: !usr/bin/env/python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: minitrino.components.Environment

methods.name: minitrino.components.Environment._get_minitrino_lib_dir
documentation:
Gets the library directory. The directory can be determined in the
following order of precedence:

1. Pass `LIB_PATH` via env (`--env` option, OS env, or Minitrino config
   file)
2. Check to see if the library exists in relation to the positioning of
   the `components.py`file and assume the project is running in a
   repository.

methods.name: minitrino.components.Environment._user_init
documentation: Initialize attributes that depend on user-provided input.

methods.name: minitrino.components.Environment._handle_minitrino_user_dir
documentation:
Checks if a Minitrino directory exists in the user home directory.
If not, it is created. The path to the Minitrino user home directory is
returned.

methods.name: minitrino.components.Environment._get_config_file
documentation:
Returns the file path of the minitrino.cfg file. Warns if the file
path regardless for future creation.

methods.name: minitrino.components.Environment._get_docker_clients
documentation:
Gets DockerClient and APIClient objects. Returns a tuple of DockerClient
and APIClient objects, respectively. Returns None types for each client
if they fail to fetch.

Provides context and core controls that are globally accessible in
command scripts. This class should not be instantiated from anywhere but the
CLI's entrypoint, as it depends on user-provided inputs.

### Public Attributes (Interactive)

- `logger`: A `minitrino.utils.Logger` object.
- `env`: An `EnvironmentVariables` object containing all CLI environment
    variables, subdivided by sections when possible.
- `modules`: A `Modules` object containing metadata about Minitrino modules.
- `cmd_executor`: A `CommandExecutor` object to execute shell commands in
  the host shell and inside containers.
- `docker_client`: A `docker.DockerClient` object.
- `api_client`: A `docker.APIClient` object.

### Public Attributes (Static)

- `verbose`: If `True`, logs flagged as verbose to are sent to stdout.
- `user_home_dir`: The home directory of the current user.
- `minitrino_user_dir`: The location of the Minitrino directory relative to
  the user home directory (~/.minitrino/).
- `config_file`: The location of the user's minitrino.cfg file.
- `snapshot_dir`: The location of the user's snapshot directory (this is
    essentially a temporary directory, as 'permanent' snapshot tarballs are
    written to the library or user-specified directory).
- `minitrino_lib_dir`: The location of the Minitrino library.

classes.name: minitrino.components.EnvironmentVariables

methods.name: minitrino.components.EnvironmentVariables._parse_user_env
documentation:
Parses user-provided environment variables for the current
command. First order of precedence.

methods.name: minitrino.components.EnvironmentVariables._parse_os_env
documentation:
Parses environment variables from the user's shell. Second order of
precedence.

methods.name: minitrino.components.EnvironmentVariables._parse_minitrino_config
documentation:
Parses the Minitrino config file and adds it to the env
dictionary. Third order of precedence.

methods.name: minitrino.components.EnvironmentVariables._parse_library_env
documentation:
Parses the Minitrino library's `minitrino.env` file. Lowest
precedence in environment variable order, loaded during
`_user_init()`.

methods.name: minitrino.components.EnvironmentVariables._log_env_vars
documentation: Logs environment variables.

Exposes all Minitrino variables.

### Parameters

- `ctx`: Instantiated Environment object (with user input already accounted
  for).

### Usage

```python # ctx object has an instantiated EnvironmentVariables object
env_variable = ctx.env.get("STARBURST_VER", "388-e")
```

classes.name: minitrino.components.Modules

methods.name: minitrino.components.Modules.get_running_modules
documentation:
Returns dict of running modules (includes container objects and
Docker labels).

methods.name: minitrino.components.Modules._load_modules
documentation: Loads module data during instantiation.

Contains information about all valid Minitrino modules.

### Parameters
- `ctx`: Instantiated Environment object (with user input already accounted
  for).

### Public Attributes
- `data`: A dictionary of module information.

### Public Methods
- `get_running_modules()`: Returns a list of running modules.

classes.name: minitrino.components.CommandExecutor

methods.name: minitrino.components.CommandExecutor.execute_commands
documentation:
Executes commands in the user's shell or inside of a container.
Returns output as well as stores the output in the `output` attribute.

### Parameters

- `args`: Commands that will be executed in the order provided.

Keyword Arguments:

- `trigger_error`: If `False`, errors (non-zero exit codes) from
  executed commands will not raise an exception. Defaults to `True`.
- `environment`: A dictionary of environment variables to pass to the
  subprocess or container.
- `suppress_output`: If `True`, output to stdout from the executed
  command will be suppressed.
- `container`: A Docker container object. If passed in, the command will
  be executed through the Docker SDK instead of the subprocess module.
- `docker_user`: The user to execute the command as in the Docker
  container (default: `root`).

### Return Values

- A list of dicts with each dict containing the following keys:
    - `command`: the original command passed to the function
    - `output`: the combined output of stdout and stderr
    - `exit_code`: the exit code of the command

methods.name: minitrino.components.CommandExecutor._execute_in_shell
documentation: Executes a command in the host shell.

methods.name: minitrino.components.CommandExecutor._execute_in_container
documentation:
Executes a command inside of a container through the Docker SDK
(similar to `docker exec`).

methods.name: minitrino.components.CommandExecutor._construct_environment
documentation:
Merges provided environment dictionary with user's shell environment
variables. For shell execution, the host environment will be set to the
existing variables in the host environment. For container execution, the
host environment will be set to the container's existing environment
variables.

methods.name: minitrino.components.CommandExecutor._strip_ansi
documentation: Strips ANSI escape sequences from strings.

Executes commands in the host shell/host containers with customized
handling of stdout/stderr output.

### Parameters

- `ctx`: Instantiated Environment object (with user input already accounted
  for).

### Public Methods

- `execute_commands()`: Executes commands in the user's shell or inside of a
    container.

Welcome to the Minitrino command line interface.

To report issues and ask questions, please file a GitHub issue and apply a
descriptive label at the GitHub repository:
https://github.com/jefflester/minitrino

modules.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_config
documentation: !usr/bin/env/python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_config.cli
documentation: Config command for Minitrino.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_config.write_template
documentation: Writes configuration template.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_config.edit_file
documentation:
Gets the editor from user configuration and passes to the Click edit
function if the value is present.

modules.name: minitrino.settings
documentation: !usr/bin/env/python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Docker labels

modules.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision
documentation: !usr/bin/env/python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Instead of using the Docker SDK, this script will form a Docker Compose
command string to execute straight through the docker compose CLI. This is
required because the Docker SDK does not support communication with Compose
files, and Minitrino benefits hugely from Docker Compose.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.cli
documentation:
Provision command for Minitrino. If the resulting docker compose command
is unsuccessful, the function exits with a non-zero status code.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.append_running_modules
documentation:
Checks if any modules are already running. If they are, they are appended
to the provided modules list and the updated list is returned.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.check_compatibility
documentation:
Checks if any of the provided modules are mutually exclusive of each
other. If they are, a user error is raised.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.check_enterprise
documentation:
Checks if any of the provided modules are Starburst Enterprise features.
If they are, we check that a pointer to a SEP license is provided.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.check_volumes
documentation:
Removes `minitrino_catalogs` volume if it exists, and also checks if any of
the modules have persistent volumes and issues a warning to the user if so.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.chunk
documentation:
Builds docker compose command chunk for the chosen modules. Returns a
command chunk string.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.build_command
documentation:
Builds a formatted docker compose command for shell execution. Returns a
docker compose command string.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.execute_bootstraps
documentation:
Executes bootstrap script for each container that has one––bootstrap
scripts will only execute once the container is fully running to prevent
conflicts with procedures executing as part of the container's entrypoint.
After each script executes, the relevant container is added to a restart
list.

Returns a list of containers names which had bootstrap scripts executed
inside of them.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.execute_container_bootstrap
documentation: Executes a single bootstrap inside a container.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.get_current_trino_cfgs
documentation:
Get Trino config.properties and jvm.config files. Return the two sets of
configs as lists, e.g.:

[['a', 'b'], ['c', 'd'], ['e', 'f']]

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.write_trino_cfg
documentation:
Appends Trino config from various modules if specified in the module YAML
file. The Trino container is added to the restart list if any configs are
written to files in the container.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.check_dup_cfgs
documentation:
Checks for duplicate configs in Trino config files (jvm.config and
config.properties). This is a safety check for modules that may improperly
modify these files.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.restart_containers
documentation: Restarts all the containers in the list.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.provision_workers
documentation:
Provisions (or updates) workers.

Scenarios:

1. No workers are specified and no workers are running: do nothing.
2. The user specifies a worker count and no workers exist: the specified
   number of workers are provisioned.
3. The user does not specify a worker count, but there are active workers:
   the worker count will be set to the number of running workers.
4. The user specifies a worker count greater than the number of active
   workers: the worker count will increase to the specified number.
5. The user specifies a worker count less than the number of active workers:
   the worker with the highest iterator is removed from the network.

I am enumerating all cases for my own sanity.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_provision.rollback
documentation: Rolls back the provisioning command in the event of an error.

modules.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot
documentation: !usr/bin/env/python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.cli
documentation: Snapshot command for Minitrino.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.validate_name
documentation: Validates the chosen filename for correct input.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.check_exists
documentation:
Checks if the resulting tarball exists. If it exists, the user is
prompted to overwrite the existing file.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.prepare_snapshot_dir
documentation:
Checks if the snapshot temp directory exists. If it does, clears
files/directories inside of it. If it doesn't, (1) creates it and clones the
library structure, (2) adds a Bash file that can be executed to spin up the
environment as it was snapshotted.

Returns the absolute path of the named snapshot directory.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.build_snapshot_command
documentation:
Builds a basic shell command that can be used to provision an environment
with the minitrino CLI. Used for snapshot purposes.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.build_command_string
documentation:
Builds a command string that can be used to create an environment with
the designated modules.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.create_snapshot_command_file
documentation:
Creates an .sh file in the minitrino directory for usage by the snapshot
command. This way, a similar command used to provision the environment is
preserved.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.clone_lib_dir
documentation:
Clones the library directory structure and necessary top-level files in
preparation for copying over module directories.

Returns the absolute path of the named snapshot directory.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.handle_copy_config_file
documentation:
Handles the copying of the user config file to the named snapshot
directory. Calls `scrub_config_file()` if `no_scrub` is True.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.copy_config_file
documentation: Copies user config file to the named snapshot directory.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.scrub_config_file
documentation: Scrubs the user config file of sensitive data.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.scrub_line
documentation: Scrubs a line from a snapshot config file. Returns the scrubbed line.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.copy_module_dirs
documentation: Copies module directories into the named snapshot directory.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.create_named_tarball
documentation:
Creates a tarball of the named snapshot directory and placed in the
library's snapshot directory.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.snapshot_runner
documentation: Executes sequential snapshot command functions.

functions.name: minitrino.cmd.cmd_snapshot.check_complete
documentation:
Checks if the snapshot completed. If detected as incomplete, exists with
a non-zero status code.
